# Patterns excluded from `rsync --delete` deploys.
#
# These patterns serve two purposes:
#   1. Files matched here are NOT transferred from the runner.
#   2. Files matched here are NOT removed on the destination by --delete.
#
# Anything that should live only on the server (env files, runtime
# logs, server-managed state) MUST be listed here so it isn't wiped
# on deploy.

# --- VCS / CI / agent metadata ---
.git/
.github/
.gitignore
.dockerignore
.serena/
.serena.local.bak/
.semgrep/
.superpowers/
.worktrees/
.claude/

# --- IDE / local tooling ---
.vscode/
.idea/
.phpcs.cache

# --- Tests, dev-only sources ---
e2e/
docs/
playwright.config.ts
playwright-report/
test-results/

# --- Build / dev configuration with no runtime role ---
phpcs.xml
.markdownlint.yml
Dockerfile

# --- Node / Bun (only used for E2E tests + markdown linting) ---
node_modules/
package.json
bun.lock

# --- Project meta files ---
CLAUDE.md
CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
README.md

# --- TRACKED EXAMPLE FILE (transferred + overwritten every deploy) ---
# The "+" prefix is an rsync include-override: this rule must precede the
# matching ".env.*" exclude below for first-match-wins to honor it. We
# intentionally DO want .env.example to ship and overwrite the server's
# copy on every deploy so admins can diff it against .env.production to
# spot new or deprecated environment variables.
+ .env.example

# --- SERVER-MANAGED FILES (never transferred, never --delete) ---
# .env / .env.production carry the live secrets. .well-known/ holds
# Plesk-managed Let's Encrypt ACME challenge files — deleting it can
# break certificate renewal. Both stay untouched by rsync.
.env
.env.*
/.well-known/

# --- RUNTIME DIRECTORIES (server-side state, created pre-rsync) ---
# Whole-directory excludes: rsync neither transfers nor --delete-s these
# paths. logs/ accumulates runtime logs; results/*.json are stored test
# runs written by results.php; tmp/restart.txt is the php-fpm reload
# sentinel consumed by a root-side watcher (see deploy.yaml). All three
# directories are created by the "Ensure runtime dirs exist" SSH step
# before rsync runs, so they need not ship from the repo.
/logs/
/results/
/tmp/
